title: leonohebe hectorii
intro: Leonohebe hectorii, also known as Hector's leonohebe, is a flowering plant species endemic to New Zealand. It belongs to the family Plantaginaceae and is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial with white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Leonohebe hectorii can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which may offer health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
